title: Spergularia gracilis
taxonomy: Spergularia gracilis is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. This family is commonly known as the pink or carnation family due to the appearance of its flowers. The genus Spergularia is a group of plants that are annual or perennial and have delicate white or pink flowers. Spergularia gracilis is native to parts of Europe and Western Asia, and it is also found in other regions as a non-native species. It is often found in moist environments such as meadows and along streams.

intro: Spergularia gracilis, also known as Slender Sandspurry, is a type of annual or perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as digestive issues and skin problems.
